title: Reporting
summary: Turns raw work, data, and project context into documents a person actually wants to read — reports that lead with the conclusion and back claims with evidence, and proposals/quotes grounded in real capabilities from the second-brain rather than invented claims.
problem: Reports and proposals are slow to write and often unreadable — buried conclusions, filler, unsupported claims, or made-up capabilities. This produces skimmable, evidence-backed, audience-fit output fast.

skills.name: human-readable-report
skills.purpose: Write a status update, summary, analysis, retro, or briefing that leads with the conclusion, structures for skimming, and cuts filler — adapted to audience and language.

skills.name: proposal-writer
skills.purpose: Draft a client proposal, quote, or pitch — problem, scope, deliverables, timeline, price, assumptions, CTA — grounded in real second-brain/project context.
